title: Burr Nolyds

{{Quote|One cannot simultaneously ''save'' the Empire and give it ''away'' to the Rebels!|Burr Nolyds|<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />}}
'''Burr Nolyds''' was a wealthy and arrogant [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Noble/Legends|aristocrat]] who briefly ruled the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] as head of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Interim Ruling Council]]. He originally ascended the Empire's [[Politics/Legends|political]] ladder after supplying the organization with important resources, and his desire for power led him to support former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guard]] [[Carnor Jax]]'s plot to [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and seize the Imperial throne. Following Palpatine's [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|death]], Nolyds sat on the Ruling Council of Jax's [[Crimson Empire]], and when Jax was later [[Duel in The Squall|killed]] by his fellow former Royal Guard [[Kir Kanos]], Nolyds took over as acting head of the council and ruler of the Empire.

Ruling from a [[Council Tower|villa]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Cantrell/Legends|Ord Cantrell's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[De-Purteen]], Nolyds held tightly to his power and opposed Councilor [[Feena D'Asta (clone)|Feena D'Asta's]] suggestion that the crumbling Empire make peace with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], their opponents in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. His rule proved to be short-lived when he was [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] by [[Nom Anor]], an ally of the scheming Councilor [[Xandel Carivus]]; Nolyds's death came in the form of a [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destructing]] [[message disk]] that Anor had left in his [[Burr Nolyds's chambers|chambers]]. The shaken council responded by electing the seemingly-incompetent Carivus to succeed him so they could pull Carivus's strings and investigate Nolyds's murder from behind the scenes.

==Biography==
===Ascent===
{{Quote|And I would ''remind'' the council… that each and every one of us has ''profited'' from the sudden vacuum left by Jax's and Wessel's untimely deaths… just as we did from Palpatine's.|Burr Nolyds, to the Interim Ruling Council|<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />}}
[[File:Burr.jpg|thumb|left|125px|Burr Nolyds assumed leadership of the Galactic Empire after Carnor Jax's death.]]
Burr Nolyds was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" /> [[Noble/Legends|aristocrat]] who lived during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He used his [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|wealth]] to supply the Empire with important resources, which helped him rise in the organization's [[Politics/Legends|political]] ranks<ref name="Handbook">[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 2: ''Crimson Empire'']] {{C|under "The Imperial Interim Ruling Council"}}</ref> and join the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]]<ref name="CSWE" /> by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The council had gained considerable power within the Empire following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]; by 10 ABY,<ref name="NEC" /> they had allied with [[Carnor Jax]], an ambitious member of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]]. When Palpatine [[Transfer essence|returned]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and resumed control of the Empire<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> in that year,<ref name="NEC" /> Jax plotted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him and seize power.<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> Nolyds, unhappy with the tight control that the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] kept on his supporters, supported the coup.<ref name="CSWE" />

The rest of the council was similarly determined to maintain their hard-won authority, and together the councilors bribed the [[Emperor's physician]] to sabotage Palpatine's various clone bodies.<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> Palpatine was in the midst of [[Operation Shadow Hand|leading]] the [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]] of his [[Dark Empire]] in their [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and when his final body was [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|destroyed]] by his enemies<ref name="Empire's End II">[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]]</ref> in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Jax seized the Imperial throne.<ref name="NEC" /> Nolyds served on his Interim Ruling Council,<ref name="CSWE" /> a thirteen-member committee that ruled Jax's [[Crimson Empire]] with the former Royal Guard at its head. However, the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial fleet]] was left confused without orders from their Emperor, and their retreat from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] caused the Empire to quickly crumble. Many [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] refused to follow the dictates of the Interim Ruling Council and broke away, forming various [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] holdings. The Crimson Empire's territory was thus limited to a narrow band of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that stretched from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] to the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]<ref name="NEC" />—the only territory still answerable to centralized Imperial control.<ref name="TEA" />

Based on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ord Cantrell/Legends|Ord Cantrell]],<ref name="TEA" /> Nolyds<ref name="CSWE" /> and the rest of the council governed from a [[Council Tower|villa]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[De-Purteen]]. They passed many decisions down to the Empire's remaining [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]], however.<ref name="TEA" /> Jax had some councilors who had planned to expose his coup killed,<ref name="Handbook" /> and Councilor [[Sarcev Quest]], one of Jax's closest allies, exercised several abuses of power and had any dissenters [[Prisoner/Legends|incarcerated]].<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> Later in 11 ABY,<ref name="NEC" /> Jax sought out fellow former Royal Guard [[Kir Kanos]], believing him to be a threat to the Empire. Kanos [[Duel in The Squall|murdered]] Jax on the planet [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] in a bout of revenge for Palpatine's death,<ref name="Handbook Jax">[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 2: ''Crimson Empire'']] {{C|under "Carnor Jax"}}</ref> and the council was thrown into chaos.<ref name="Handbook" /> They immediately moved against Quest, and had him [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] for a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] before dumping him onto the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> Nolyds took over as acting head of the council<ref name="Handbook" /> and ruler of the Empire.<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="Handbook" />

===Death and fallout===
{{Quote|What's this? A ''message disk''? Why couldn't they leave it on the table like ''civilized'' people? Hmm… I wonder who sent it. I don't recognize the ''crest''…|Nolyds's last words|<ref name="Crimson Empire II 2">[[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 2|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:Burrdeath.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Nolyds's final seconds]]
When Nolyds came into power,<ref name="Handbook" /> the New Republic had recently been striking at the western flanks of the Empire's territory<ref name="TEA" /> and had [[Battle of Phaeda|captured]] the planet [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]].<ref name="Crimson Empire 5">[[Crimson Empire 5|''Crimson Empire'' 5]]</ref> On Ord Cantrell, Nolyds hosted a meeting of the council in a courtyard of their villa, where Councilor [[Feena D'Asta (clone)|Feena D'Asta]] suggested that the only way to rebuild the Empire in the face of New Republic pressure was to sue for peace. Nolyds in turn accused her of trying to split the council and increase her own power; he then reminded her that every present councilor already had considerable power due to having followed Jax, and adjourned the meeting to retire to his [[Burr Nolyds's chambers|chambers]].<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />

However, a bomb secured inside of a [[message disk]] had been placed on Nolyds's chamber floor<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" /> by [[Nom Anor]], an ally of Councilor [[Xandel Carivus]] who was aiding Carivus's plans to become the new head of the council.<ref name="Handbook Nom">[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 2: ''Crimson Empire'']] {{C|under "Nom Anor"}}</ref> Unknown to even Carivus was the fact that Anor was an advance [[Executor (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|agent]] of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]], and was targeting the council to hasten the Empire's collapse in advance of a massive [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> The message disk [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destructed]] when Nolyds activated it, and he was killed in the ensuing explosion. Shaken by the death, Councilors [[Mahd Windcaller]] and [[Norym Kim]] nominated Carivus to succeed Nolyds, figuring him to be a spineless [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrat]] who could become the next target of their unknown [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]]. Meanwwhile, they would control events and investigate Nolyds's murder from behind the scenes. When they swayed other councilors to their side, Carivus was elected by a majority vote.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />

Nolyds's death was investigated by the Empire's [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Ganner]], who found Kir Kanos's Royal Guard crest on the message disk,<ref name="Crimson Empire II 2" /> evidence that had been planted by Anor.<ref name="Handbook Nom" /> When Ganner found the same crest on the [[Knife/Legends|knife]] that Anor used to kill his next victim, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Llon Banjeer]], the Lieutenant suggested to the council that they were being targeted by Kanos, whom Jax had predicted would come after them. The [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Immodet]] formed his own conclusion, accusing the council's [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] members of exacting a vendetta against their Human peers.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 2" /> A power-hungry Carivus took advantage of the discord and used the murders as justification to establish his own [[Xandel Carivus's Private Guard|private guard]], whom he promptly had arrest every non-Human councilor.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 3">[[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 3]]</ref> With the council severely depleted, Carivus disbanded it and declared himself the new Emperor.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 5">[[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 5|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 5]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|No need to ''translate'', you metal toady. It's clear to all of us what Councilwoman D'Asta is trying to do. It's a blessing her respected ''father'' is too ''ill'' to join us. Seeing this transparent ploy of hers might ''kill'' him! Isn't it clear? She's trying to ''split'' the council and increase her ''own'' power!|Nolyds, to Ch'Unkk's protocol droid and the Interim Ruling Council|<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />}}
[[File:Greengreeneyes.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Burr Nolyds, arrogant aristocrat]]
Burr Nolyds was an arrogant aristocrat who used his wealth to push himself into Imperial power.<ref name="Handbook" /> Although a staunch Imperial loyalist,<ref name="CSWE Interim Ruling Council">{{CSWECite|II|91|Imperial Interim Ruling Council}}</ref> he disliked being kept on a short leash by Palpatine and supported Carnor Jax's plot to kill the Emperor.<ref name="CSWE" /> To Nolyds, Kir Kanos's continued loyalty to Palpatine after the Emperor's death was misguided patriotism. Nevertheless, he thought Jax foolish for personally seeking out Kanos rather than dispatching [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] against him.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" /> Following Jax's death, Nolyds looked after his own interests and sought to gain control over his fellow councilors.<ref name="Handbook" />

As the ruler of the Empire, Nolyds desired to hold on to his power. He opposed Feena D'Asta's suggestion that peace be sought with the New Republic, asserting that it would be tantamount to giving the Empire away to their foes. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of his death, Nolyds had made many enemies. He publicly accused D'Asta of trying to increase her own power and dismissed the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] Councilor [[Ch'Unkk]]'s [[Ch'Unkk's protocol droid|translator droid]] as a "metal toady."<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" /> During his brief rule over the Empire, Nolyds was a gaunt, [[Color/Legends|white]]-[[Hair/Legends|haired]] man<ref name="CSWE" /> with light skin and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He wore a set of blue and green [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] and talked to himself while alone, muttering that whoever had left the message disk in his chambers was uncivilized due to having left it on the floor rather than the table.<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Burr Nolyds first appeared in the [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic miniseries]] ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood]]''. The issue was written by [[Mike Richardson]] and [[Randy Stradley]], illustrated by [[Paul Gulacy]], and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Crimson Empire II 1" /> on [[November 11]], [[1998]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54045/star_wars_crimson_empire_ii_-_council_of_blood_1998_1|text=Star Wars: Crimson Empire II - Council of Blood (1998) #1}}</ref>
